untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tracking downside-to-upside volume ratio (NYSE breadth indicator) and counting occurrences of threshold violations over a rolling window. The allowed indicator set includes vol_ratio_20 but does not include: (1) a downside volume breadth ratio indicator, (2) a mechanism to count discrete threshold-crossing events within a time window, or (3) stateful event counters. This is a multi-occurrence pattern-detection rule that cannot be expressed as simple point-in-time conditions using the available indicators and operators.
translation_notes: ARONSON_DRY_0_0 is an event-counting signal that requires: (a) measuring upside vs downside volume on NYSE (not available in standard OHLCV indicators), (b) detecting when vol_ratio > 11.0, (c) counting how many times this occurs in last 120 days, and (d) triggering when count >= 3. The backtesting framework would need custom breadth indicators and event accumulation logic. Original source mentions threshold=9 with 2 instances in 3 months; this variant uses 11.0 with 3 instances in 4 months.
